meta.title: Original Wave Optics Practice Question Bank
meta.chapter: NCERT Class 12 Physics Chapter 10 - Wave Optics
meta.licenseNote: Original educational questions generated for this project. No copied coaching material or copyrighted question banks.

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Constructive Interference?
questions.answer: Delta x = n lambda
questions.solution: Constructive Interference: Bright fringe condition. Therefore the correct relation is Delta x = n lambda.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Destructive Interference?
questions.answer: Delta x = (2n+1)lambda/2
questions.solution: Destructive Interference: Dark fringe condition. Therefore the correct relation is Delta x = (2n+1)lambda/2.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Phase-Path Relation?
questions.answer: Delta phi = 2 pi Delta x/lambda
questions.solution: Phase-Path Relation: Converts path difference into phase. Therefore the correct relation is Delta phi = 2 pi Delta x/lambda.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents YDSE Bright Condition?
questions.answer: d sin theta = n lambda
questions.solution: YDSE Bright Condition: Angular bright fringe condition. Therefore the correct relation is d sin theta = n lambda.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Fringe Width?
questions.answer: beta = lambda D/d
questions.solution: Fringe Width: Spacing between consecutive fringes. Therefore the correct relation is beta = lambda D/d.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Angular Fringe Width?
questions.answer: theta = lambda/d
questions.solution: Angular Fringe Width: Angular separation of fringes. Therefore the correct relation is theta = lambda/d.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Interference Intensity?
questions.answer: I = I1 + I2 + 2sqrt(I1I2)cos phi
questions.solution: Interference Intensity: Intensity from coherent superposition. Therefore the correct relation is I = I1 + I2 + 2sqrt(I1I2)cos phi.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Equal Source Intensity?
questions.answer: I = 4I0 cos^2(phi/2)
questions.solution: Equal Source Intensity: Interference intensity for equal slits. Therefore the correct relation is I = 4I0 cos^2(phi/2).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Fringe Shift by Sheet?
questions.answer: shift = (mu-1)tD/d
questions.solution: Fringe Shift by Sheet: Extra optical path shifts pattern. Therefore the correct relation is shift = (mu-1)tD/d.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Single Slit Minima?
questions.answer: a sin theta = m lambda
questions.solution: Single Slit Minima: Diffraction minima condition. Therefore the correct relation is a sin theta = m lambda.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Central Maximum Width?
questions.answer: width = 2 lambda D/a
questions.solution: Central Maximum Width: Width of central diffraction maximum. Therefore the correct relation is width = 2 lambda D/a.
questions.commonTrap: This is central maximum, not fringe width.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Rayleigh Criterion?
questions.answer: theta_min = 1.22 lambda/D
questions.solution: Rayleigh Criterion: Minimum angular separation resolved. Therefore the correct relation is theta_min = 1.22 lambda/D.
questions.commonTrap: D is aperture, not screen distance.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Malus Law?
questions.answer: I = I0 cos^2 theta
questions.solution: Malus Law: Intensity through analyzer. Therefore the correct relation is I = I0 cos^2 theta.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Brewster Law?
questions.answer: tan iB = mu
questions.solution: Brewster Law: Angle for complete polarization by reflection. Therefore the correct relation is tan iB = mu.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable
